name: Beleña Reservoir and Tamajón
name_original: Embalse de Beleña y Tamajón — circular desde Espinosa de Henares
description: This moderate 37.7-mile road cycling route from Espinosa de Henares offers scenic views around the Beleña Reservoir.
region_intro: Road cycling routes Espinosa De Henares traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, indicative of hilly and mountainous terrain. The region features a mix of open countryside, historic villages, and natural landmarks such as reservoirs and peaks. Road cyclists can expect varied routes that include both challenging climbs and scenic stretches through rural areas. Best road cycling routes around Espinosa De Henares The most popular road cycling route is Black Villages of Guadalajara Route, a 53.5 miles (86.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historic villages with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Beleña Reservoir and Tamajón, a moderate 37.7 miles (60.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Beleña Reservoir and passes through the village of Tamajón. Local road cyclists also love the View of Cogolludo – El Picoz, Alto Rey Peak loop from Espinosa de Henares, an 83.4 miles (134.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with views towards El Picoz and Alto Rey Peak, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Espinosa De Henares is defined by significant climbs, reservoir views, and routes connecting historic villages. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Espinosa De Henares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Espinosa De Henares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are there in Espinosa De Henares?
A: Espinosa De Henares offers over 30 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult trails to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What are the road cycling conditions like in Espinosa De Henares?
A: Road cycling in Espinosa De Henares is characterized by significant elevation changes, indicating hilly and mountainous terrain. Cyclists can expect varied routes that include challenging climbs and scenic stretches through rural areas, often with views of reservoirs and historic villages.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Espinosa De Henares?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's hilly and mountainous terrain suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Espinosa De Henares?
A: The routes around Espinosa De Henares are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes listed. Beginners might find the terrain challenging due to the significant elevation changes and climbs.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced road cyclists expect in Espinosa De Henares?
A: Advanced road cyclists will find plenty of challenges, with 17 difficult routes featuring substantial elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina – View of the Alcorlo Reservoir loop from Espinosa de Henares&lt;/a&gt; covers over 150 km with nearly 2,900 meters of ascent, offering a demanding ride.

Q: Are there loop road cycling routes available around Espinosa De Henares?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are loops. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cogolludo – El Picoz, Alto Rey Peak loop from Espinosa de Henares&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 134.2 km ride through mountainous terrain.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Espinosa De Henares?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Espinosa De Henares is a small town, and street parking or designated parking areas within the town are generally available, especially near the start points of routes.

Q: Can I reach road cycling routes in Espinosa De Henares using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Espinosa De Henares are limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting points of the routes, as the region is more accessible by car.

Q: What scenic views or natural landmarks can I expect on road cycling routes in Espinosa De Henares?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural scenery. You can expect views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the Beleña Reservoir, as well as panoramic vistas from elevated points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cogolludo&lt;/a&gt; and towards Alto Rey Peak.

Q: Are there any historic villages or points of interest to explore along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017140650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Black Villages of Guadalajara Route&lt;/a&gt; explores historic villages. You can also pass through or near towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita&lt;/a&gt; and see landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6594742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista of Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Espinosa De Henares?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the significant climbs, the rewarding reservoir views, and the routes that connect charming historic villages, offering a blend of physical challenge and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes on routes are not detailed, many routes pass through or near villages such as Tamajón, Hita, and Jadraque. These villages typically offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and take a break.

Q: Are there less crowded road bike trails in Espinosa De Henares?
A: Espinosa De Henares is generally a less densely populated area compared to major cities, suggesting that many of its road cycling routes offer a quieter experience. The focus on rural landscapes and smaller villages often means less traffic and more serene rides.
tips: 10th century bridge over the Henares River created during the Caliphate of Guadalajara for the passage of trade to Zaragoza. Today the river landscape can provide you with shade and a place to rest on your journey.
A road that goes down to the town of Fuencemillán and where you can see the whole of the town!

The primitive church was built in the 13th century and was Romanesque. It was enlarged in the 16th century by order of the Mendoza family, already in a Renaissance style. It is well preserved, so it is a perfect example to realize the characteristic color that the Tamajón stone takes.

The Beleña reservoir built on the Sorbe river offers a very picturesque landscape of this area of Guadalajara. From the top of the dam you can have beautiful views of the reservoir with the Ocejón in the background, which rises to 2049 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it doesn't feature extreme mountain passes, you can expect continuous changes in elevation with approximately 872 meters of ascent over its 60.7 km distance. It's suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a good workout.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows secondary roads with low traffic, characteristic of the Guadalajara province. You'll experience open landscapes and generally quiet roads. The terrain involves gentle to moderate slopes, offering a tranquil cycling experience through picturesque views.

faqs.question: What sights will I see along the Embalse de Beleña y Tamajón road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the historic Stone Bridge over the Henares River, scenic View of Cogolludo, the charming Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, and the expansive Beleña Reservoir.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Guadalajara region, where this route is located, is best enjoyed during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, including potential lavender fields. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder conditions, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Espinosa de Henares. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town center or main access points.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its 60.7 km distance and moderate difficulty with significant elevation gain, this route is generally more suited for experienced cyclists. While some sections might be manageable, the full loop might be challenging for younger children or families looking for a very easy ride. Consider shorter, flatter sections if cycling with family.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads, dogs are generally permitted. However, they must be kept on a leash if you stop in villages or natural areas, and always under control. Be mindful of traffic and local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the way?
faqs.answer: You will find opportunities for refreshments in the villages along the route, such as Espinosa de Henares at the start/end, and potentially in Tamajón. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between villages.

faqs.question: Does this route require any special permits or have entrance fees?
faqs.answer: No, this road cycling route does not require any special permits or entrance fees. It traverses public roads and natural areas, including parts of the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, which does not have an entrance fee for general access. Always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for cycling within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara?
faqs.answer: When cycling within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, it's important to stick to marked roads and trails. Respect wildlife, do not litter, and avoid disturbing the natural environment. While there are no specific cycling permits required for this road route, always be mindful of other road users and local conservation efforts.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino del Arcipreste de Hita, Pueblos de la Arquitectura Negra (Etapa 3 and Etapa 4), Camino de Cogolludo, Camino San Isidro, Antigua carretera Fuencemillán - Cogolludo, Camino de la Cabra, Camino del Sotillo, Camino de la Dehesa GU-188, and Camino de Jócar.
